disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden severe pain, heat, redness and swelling of the ear evolving over about 6 hours
trigger_question_examples: What's going on with your ear?
How quickly did this come on?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My right ear came on suddenly tonight — over about six hours it's gone bright red, hot, hugely swollen and it's incredibly painful.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The inflammation involves the firm upper part of the ear; the soft fleshy lobe at the bottom looks and feels normal
trigger_question_examples: Does the swelling involve the soft earlobe at the bottom, or just the firm upper part?
If you look in a mirror, does the fleshy lobe look normal or is it also red and swollen?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that you mention it — the soft dangly lobe at the bottom looks completely normal, not red or swollen at all. It's the firm upper part of my ear that's all red and puffy.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever reported; home thermometer reads 37.0 degrees
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any fevers or chills?
Have you been able to take your temperature?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever — I checked and my thermometer said 37.0. I don't feel hot or shivery anywhere else, just the ear.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No purulent discharge from the ear canal and no change in hearing
trigger_question_examples: Is there any pus or fluid draining from inside the ear?
Has your hearing changed on that side?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nothing is draining out of the ear and my hearing seems the same as normal.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No recent ear piercing, trauma or injury to the ear
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any recent ear piercing, a knock or an injury to the ear?
Any recent cuts, insect bites or trauma to that ear?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No piercings, no injury, nothing like that — it just came on by itself.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Prior similar self-limiting episodes of ear cartilage inflammation
trigger_question_examples: Have you had episodes of ear or cartilage inflammation in the past?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — my ear cartilage has flared up and got inflamed like this once or twice before. It settled on its own or with anti-inflammatories, but it's never been this severe.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Patient is highly distressed and fears permanent ear deformity and a spreading brain infection
trigger_question_examples: What are you most worried about?
How are you coping with this tonight?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm really frightened — I think it's a bad infection that could spread to my brain, or that my ear will be permanently deformed like a cauliflower ear.
